name: Classic Mexican Flan
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, melt sugar over moderate heat, stirring frequently until it becomes a dark, caramel-colored liquid. Remove from heat and pour into a 4-cup metal ring mold or 8 individual molds. Quickly turn the mold to coat the bottom and sides with the caramel. Let cool so the caramel hardens.
2. Preheat the oven to 325°F (163°C).
3. In an electric blender, combine condensed milk, milk, egg yolks, eggs, almond extract, and vanilla extract. Blend until well mixed.
4. Pour the mixture into the mold(s) placed in a larger pan filled with water to a depth of about 1/2 inch. Bake for 1 hour.
5. Remove from the oven and let cool completely. Refrigerate up to 2 days.
6. To serve, cover the mold with an inverted serving platter. Hold both the mold and platter together and carefully turn them over. Lift off the mold. The caramel will fall as a liquid sauce over the custard.
